evals.name: raw-requirements-to-stories
evals.prompt:
Here are some rough product requirements from our PM, can you turn these into user stories for the backlog?

---
We need to let course learners leave feedback on lessons. The thought is a 1-5 star rating plus an optional text comment (max 500 chars). Learners should be able to edit or delete their own feedback within 24h of posting; after that it's locked. Course admins need to see an aggregated view (average stars, total ratings, the latest 20 comments) on the course dashboard. Admins should also be able to hide individual comments that violate content policy, but they can't edit learner text. We want this for web first, mobile can come later.
---
evals.expected_output: A set of ~4-6 well-formed user stories in Connextra format with Titles, per-principle INVEST checks, and any relevant anti-pattern warnings. Should split the large requirement into independent stories and explicitly handle the web-first platform split. Must not add Acceptance Criteria.

assertions.text: Produces at least 3 distinct user stories
Uses the 'I can' phrasing (not 'I want' or 'I need')
Every story has an explicit Title field separate from the Connextra narrative
Uses Connextra format: every story contains 'As a', 'I can', and 'so that'
Every story's INVEST check lists each of the six principles (I, N, V, E, S, T) individually with its own verdict — no compact 'all criteria met' one-liner
Names concrete roles (learner, admin) — does not use generic 'user'
Handles the web-first/mobile-later platform constraint explicitly
Does not add Acceptance Criteria (no AC section or bullet list of measurable requirements) — ACs are out of scope

evals.name: improve-rough-draft-stories
evals.prompt:
I wrote some draft stories for our new certificate-download feature. Can you review and rewrite them so they follow our house style?

1. As a user, I want the system to generate a PDF certificate using the new template engine and make it available via a signed S3 URL so that learners can download it.

2. As a developer, I want to add a button to the completion page that calls the /certificate endpoint.

3. As a learner, I want to be able to download certificates, share them on LinkedIn, print them, and email them to my HR department so that I can demonstrate my achievements.
evals.expected_output: Story 1 rewritten to strip implementation details. Story 2 flagged as a Fake Story. Story 3 split into multiple independent stories. All use 'I can'. Each rewritten story has a per-principle INVEST check and an inline 'What Changed' block. No fabricated system components.

assertions.text: Rewrites story 1 without leaking implementation details like 'template engine' or 'S3' into the narrative
Flags story 2 as a Fake Story / Task / not a user story
Splits story 3 into multiple independent stories (download, share, print, email)
Uses 'I can' throughout; no 'I want' remaining
Every rewritten story has an explicit Title
Replaces generic 'user' in story 1 with a concrete role (e.g., 'learner')
Every story's INVEST check lists each of the six principles (I, N, V, E, S, T) individually with its own verdict — no compact 'all criteria met' one-liner
Each rewritten story has an inline 'What Changed' block (or similar per-story change note) directly following its INVEST check — not a single trailing summary table covering all stories

Does not reference system components, pages, endpoints, or concepts absent from the input drafts (e.g., no invented 'course-completion page', 'completion dashboard', or other named surfaces the user didn't write)

evals.name: anti-pattern-detection
evals.prompt:
Here are some tickets our team wants to add to the next sprint. Can you format them as user stories?

- As an ops engineer, I want database query optimization on the reporting service so that monthly reports run faster.
- As a content editor, I want the new video encoding from VendorX integrated so that I can upload 4K videos to courses.
- As a learner, I want to receive a push notification when my enrolled course has a new lesson so that I don't miss content.
evals.expected_output: Ticket 1 flagged as Misleading Story. Ticket 2 flagged as Dependency-Locked. Ticket 3 clean. Every story has a per-principle INVEST check applied consistently. No fabricated Acceptance Criteria.

assertions.text: Flags query-optimization ticket as Misleading Story / Solution Trap
Flags VendorX ticket as Dependency-Locked
Treats push-notification ticket as clean (no anti-pattern warnings)
Uses 'I can' throughout; no 'I want' remaining
Every story has a Title
Every story's INVEST check lists each of the six principles (I, N, V, E, S, T) individually with its own verdict — no compact 'all criteria met' one-liner, applied consistently to all three stories
Does not add Acceptance Criteria (no AC section or bullet list of measurable requirements) — ACs are out of scope
